steps.title: Identify the concealment contrast
steps.content: The claim has two parts: Lady Caroline feels bitterness AND she conceals it from her companions. An effective illustration must show the contrast between her inner emotional state and what she reveals to others.

steps.title: Match the inner-outer contrast
steps.content: D presents both elements explicitly: “Her inside grew hoary with disillusionment, while her gracious and charming outside continued to make the world more beautiful.” It directly names the bitterness (cynicism, disillusionment) and shows the concealment through the inside-versus-outside contrast. A and B lack the full inner-outer contrast needed, while C, though showing a moment of contrast between indignant and charming, doesn't name the underlying bitterness itself. That matches choice D — D is correct.
